title: Quick but probably complicated question spoilers for EoE
display_title: Quick but probably complicated question spoilers for EoE
wikitext:
[]: Quick but probably complicated question *spoilers for EoE*             Quote Post by CLOVER_STEEL » Thu Oct 01, 2009 5:59 pm Okay....Why did Shinji choke Asuka at the very end of The End of Evangelion? Oh and here's a couple more I thought of off the top of my head. 1.) For those who already died, were they able to become part of the whole instrumentality thing? 2.) Why did GNR just fall apart like that and does that mean Lilith has just turned into the soup? 3.) Why did the mass produced Evas get what looked to be Rei's face? 4.) Why did they kill themselves? 5.) Is Eva Unit 01 going to be up there forever?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Oct 01, 2009 6:02 pm Ah, questions as old as time. I'll answer #5. Most likely. Inb4RoEsequeltheoryfanwankery

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Oct 01, 2009 6:11 pm I can't answer all of these, but probably someone else can, or can point you to other threads where they've been answered. Main question: That's as up for debate as anything else, but I think the usual consensus is somewhere along the lines of this: Shinji was completely alone for weeks before anyone else returned. When Asuka came back, he flipped out due to some combination of having totally lost his mind from the isolation, from possibly not even being sure anymore whether he's still in Instrumentality or if he's alive at all, and from his last memories of Asuka being her having so thoroughly and hurtfully rejected him (which had finally pushed him over the edge to initiating the Tangification process to begin with). That's what I think, at least. And there's a thread currently going on here that goes into some of the many possible interpretations of why she came back, why she caressed his cheek, and some of the other related stuff that's so open to interpretation in that scene. I know what I think it means, but you'd probably get 10 different answers if you asked 10 different people from this forum. :P For your other questions, of what I can answer myself: 1) Presumably so, particularly if you consider the TV ending ("EoTV") to be supplementary to EoE, in which we somehow even see Kaji in Instrumentality despite his having died several weeks prior. The actual cutoff point for "how dead is too dead" is anyone's guess, I suppose. 5) It would seem so, based on what Yui said about it.

[]: Re: Quick but probably complicated question *spoilers for Eo             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 01, 2009 6:37 pm CLOVER_STEEL wrote: Okay....Why did Shinji choke Asuka at the very end of The End of Evangelion? Oh and here's a couple more I thought of off the top of my head. 1.) For those who already died, were they able to become part of the whole instrumentality thing? 2.) Why did GNR just fall apart like that and does that mean Lilith has just turned into the soup? 3.) Why did the mass produced Evas get what looked to be Rei's face? 4.) Why did they kill themselves? 5.) Is Eva Unit 01 going to be up there forever?

[]: Re: Quick but probably complicated question *spoilers for Eo             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Oct 01, 2009 6:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 2) Lilith fell apart because Instrumentality was rejected and had ended;

[]: Re: Quick but probably complicated question *spoilers for Eo             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Oct 02, 2009 2:24 am Mr. Tines wrote: CLOVER_STEEL wrote: Okay....Why did Shinji choke Asuka at the very end of The End of Evangelion?

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Oct 02, 2009 10:43 am Oh for petes sake. @ CLOVER_STEEL Don't discourage new discussion on an old topic just because we've done it before. You're a fresh set of eyes and could be enlightening. > Gets up on his well worn soap box < Thats why I hate that anime nation archive board ( even if no one liked from it this time )it holds up old conversations on things up as " the definitive analysis " on a given topic. For real people this is a tv show not rocket science. Let not make new people feel like they have to re read the last 5 years of posts to know whats going on. This is a message board people are meant to talk about things.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Oct 02, 2009 4:29 pm Yeah. I too am bothered when folks are shamed for bringing up an old topic. You can always get fresh new enlightenment when its brought up again after a prolonged period of time

If a discussion is going to start, it's better if it's in an existing thread. It would be more useful than a thread with 4 or 5 different discussions at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Sun Oct 18, 2009 7:41 pm yeah i have nothing against starting a new topic either, but if people want to bring up old topics to explain the questions in new ones why not filter out all the off-topic bullshit that ends up in thos threads such as pointless arguments of little things. its annoying trying to read huge topics in orger to get a straight answer and it seems you are getting somewhere, then there are 2+ pages of arguments and flames and you feel like you have gotten no where. what i mean is, instead of just linking, clean up the topic and sticky it or make a seperate board for it or something
